purposes.description: Extract structured medical entities (conditions, medications, procedures, lab values, vital signs) from unstructured clinical text and output them as JSON objects with type, negation status, section, and position fields

primitives.evidence: Skill provides Python regex patterns (MED_PATTERN, LAB_PATTERN) and a full extract_entities() algorithm using re, json stdlib modules
Complex regex patterns for medications, lab values, and vital signs with groups, alternation, and anchors
Output is a JSON array of entity objects with nested fields (name, dose, unit, route, frequency, start_pos, end_pos)
Must detect negation scope within clauses, identify section headers, classify entities by type, and reconcile medication lists
Output must conform to specific JSON schema with exact field names and types per entity type
Multiple constraints: negation triggers must be respected, entity types must be from allowed values, dose/value must be numbers not strings, boolean negated field

purposes.description: Compare two medication lists (admission vs discharge) and classify changes as continued, increased, decreased, discontinued, new, or switched (including same-class detection)

primitives.evidence: Requires implementing comparison logic across two parsed medication lists with drug class groupings
Multi-constraint classification: must match drug names, compare doses, detect same-class switches across 6 drug class groupings
Output classification of each medication change
